description_long: This object is a large, industrial pumping station complex set into a cross-section of earth, featuring a prominent cooling tower, several auxiliary buildings, and a network of pipes and power lines. The main structure is a gray hyperboloid cooling tower, surrounded by smaller white and gray utility buildings and a road leading to the facility. High-voltage transmission lines supported by lattice towers run parallel to the site, indicating the station's role in energy or water management. On one side, blue and orange pipes connect to a large blue reservoir and an underlying orange geological layer, suggesting groundwater extraction or geothermal activity. The terrain is grassy and undulating, with the facility embedded in a multi-layered earth section. The overall construction is robust, with concrete, metal, and glass as primary materials, and the site is designed for heavy-duty, industrial-scale operations.
description: A large industrial pumping station complex with a cooling tower, auxiliary buildings, power lines, and visible underground reservoirs and pipes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cooling tower, main building, and road leading into the facility, with the blue reservoir and orange geological layer partially visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the facility, the cooling tower, auxiliary buildings, and the full stretch of power lines across grassy terrain.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the cooling tower, the road, and the connection to the blue reservoir and orange geological layer on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the blue reservoir, orange geological layer, and the piping system connecting to the cooling tower and main facility.
